metadata.name: treasury
metadata.description: OCCA Treasury — TreasuryAccount, PolicyAccount, OperationsAccount, ProtocolFeeAccount on Solana

instructions.name: close_operations
docs: Close a revoked OperationsAccount and refund rent to the controlling
authority. Must be revoked first (terminal-state precondition makes
the rotation flow explicit + auditable).

accounts.name: company
docs: `controlling_authority` signer check happens in handler.

accounts.name: controlling_authority
docs: Receives rent refund.

instructions.name: disburse_discretionary
docs: Discretionary-class disbursement to an agent's receiving address.
Signed by controlling authority (= company.owner). Used for ad-hoc
agent payouts outside of recurring routine flow.
Same validation as routine except: no operations account, no rate
limit, no whitelist check. Budget check uses `discretionary_*`
counters. Fee always applies (intra-company agent destination).

instructions.name: disburse_discretionary_spl
docs: Discretionary-class disbursement of an SPL token to an agent's receiving
address. SPL sibling of `disburse_discretionary` — signed by controlling
authority, no operations account / rate limit / whitelist, budget drawn
from `discretionary_*` counters, fee always applies. Only the value-
movement leg differs (SPL `transfer_checked` CPI). See
`disburse_routine_spl` for the custody / ATA model.

accounts.name: controlling_authority
docs: Controlling Authority — must equal `CompanyAccount.owner`. Also pays
rent for any destination/fee ATA created on demand.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Agent receiving wallet — owner of `destination_token_account`.

instructions.name: disburse_privileged
docs: Privileged-class disbursement. Destination unrestricted: may be an
agent receiving address (fee applies) or any external pubkey (no fee).
Signed by controlling authority. Above-threshold amounts additionally
require the secondary signer registered in policy.
`is_agent_destination`: if `true`, caller MUST pass the agent's
`Deployment` as `deployment` and the destination must equal
`deployment.receiving_address`. Fee deducted per §11.1.
If `false`, `deployment` should be omitted; no fee applies.
No budget check — privileged class is unbudgeted by design (§3.2 has
no privileged_budget field).

accounts.name: secondary_signer
docs: Required only when `amount > policy.privileged_threshold_lamports`
AND a `secondary_signer` is registered. Pass `None` for below-threshold
disbursements. Pubkey verified against `policy.secondary_signer` in handler.

accounts.name: deployment
docs: Pass `Some(deployment_account)` when `is_agent_destination = true`.
Pass `None` for external destinations.

accounts.name: destination
docs: `deployment.receiving_address` only when `is_agent_destination = true`.

instructions.name: disburse_privileged_spl
docs: Privileged-class SPL disbursement. SPL sibling of `disburse_privileged`.
Destination unrestricted: agent receiving address (fee applies) or any
external wallet (no fee). Signed by controlling authority; above the
per-mint `privileged_threshold_per_token` entry (if configured) the
registered secondary signer is also required. No budget check
(privileged class is unbudgeted by design).
`is_agent_destination = true` ⇒ caller MUST pass the agent's
`Deployment`; destination must equal `deployment.receiving_address` and
the fee is deducted. `false` ⇒ `deployment` omitted, no fee. Tokens land
in `destination`'s ATA either way.

accounts.name: controlling_authority
docs: Controlling Authority — must equal `CompanyAccount.owner`. Also pays
rent for any destination/fee ATA created on demand.

accounts.name: secondary_signer
docs: Required only when `amount` exceeds the per-mint
`privileged_threshold_per_token` entry. Pubkey verified against
`policy.secondary_signer` in handler.

accounts.name: deployment
docs: Pass `Some(deployment)` when `is_agent_destination = true`, else `None`.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Destination wallet — owner of `destination_token_account`. Unrestricted
(privileged). Verified against `deployment.receiving_address` only when
`is_agent_destination = true`.

instructions.name: disburse_routine
docs: Routine-class disbursement to an agent's receiving address.
Signed by `OperationsAccount[Disbursement].signer` (operator-held key,
OCCA never holds the privkey). Per design §2, the FE constructs a
batched tx of multiple `disburse_routine` ixs (one per agent) and
the operator signs it manually each pay period.
Validation pipeline (§4.4 row 1 + §7):
1. Mint is in `treasury.accepted_assets`.
2. Phase 1: mint == `SOL_PSEUDO_MINT` (SPL deferred per §2).
3. Destination matches `Deployment.receiving_address` and the
Deployment belongs to the same company.
4. OperationsAccount is not revoked, not expired.
5. This ix's discriminator is in `ops.action_whitelist`.
6. Lazy-rollover both `policy.current_period_anchor` and
`ops.current_period_anchor` to current month start.
7. `ops.signatures_this_period < ops.rate_limit_per_period`.
8. `routine_budget_per_month[mint] - routine_spent_this_period[mint] >= amount + fee`.
Fee: always applies (intra-company agent destination per §11.1).
`fee = amount * agent_operating_fee_bps / 10_000`.
`gross = amount + fee` debited from treasury; `amount` to destination,
`fee` to ProtocolFeeAccount.

accounts.name: operations_signer
docs: Disbursement Wallet — operator-held only. Pubkey verified via the
`operations.signer == operations_signer.key()` constraint above.

accounts.name: deployment
docs: Registry-owned Deployment whose `receiving_address` must equal
`destination.key()`. Field decoding done in handler.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Net amount lands here. Marked `mut` so its lamports balance can be
updated by direct manipulation (treasury PDA is owned by this program,
not System, so we can't use SystemProgram::transfer).

instructions.name: disburse_routine_spl
docs: Routine-class disbursement of an SPL token (e.g. USDC) to an agent's
receiving address. SPL sibling of `disburse_routine` — identical
authorization, budget, fee, rate-limit and rollover semantics; only the
value-movement leg differs (SPL `transfer_checked` CPI instead of direct
lamport manipulation).
Custody model: the treasury PDA owns its own Associated Token Account
(`treasury_token_account`) per accepted mint; that ATA is the source of
funds. The net `amount` goes to the agent's destination ATA (derived
from `Deployment.receiving_address` + mint — the receiving_address stays
the wallet), the `fee` goes to the ProtocolFeeAccount's ATA. Both
recipient ATAs are created if missing (operator pays rent once per
agent/mint) so a payout never hard-fails on a missing ATA.
The treasury PDA signs the transfers via its seeds
(`["treasury", company]`), so OCCA/operator never holds the source
authority key — the same on-chain-policy-bounded model as the SOL path.
`mint` must be a real SPL mint (not `SOL_PSEUDO_MINT`) and must equal
the passed `mint` account; use `disburse_routine` for native SOL.

accounts.name: operations_signer
docs: Disbursement Wallet — operator-held only. Also pays rent for any
destination/fee ATA created on demand (create-if-needed).

accounts.name: deployment
docs: Registry-owned Deployment whose `receiving_address` must equal
`destination.key()`. Field decoding done in handler.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Agent receiving wallet — owner of `destination_token_account`. Matched
against `deployment.receiving_address` in handler. The wallet itself is
not touched; tokens land in its ATA.

accounts.name: mint
docs: SPL mint being disbursed (e.g. USDC). Must equal the `mint` argument
and be in `treasury.accepted_assets`.

accounts.name: treasury_token_account
docs: Treasury PDA's ATA for `mint` — the source of funds. Must already exist
and hold ≥ gross; treasury is funded out-of-band by sending tokens here.

accounts.name: destination_token_account
docs: Agent's destination ATA for `mint`. Created if missing (operator pays
rent ~0.002 SOL once per agent/mint) so payout never fails on a missing
ATA.

accounts.name: fee_token_account
docs: ProtocolFeeAccount PDA's ATA for `mint` — fee destination. Created if
missing (first fee accrued in this mint).

instructions.name: init_protocol_fee_account
docs: One-time singleton init for the protocol-wide fee accumulator. Called
once at program deployment by the upgrade authority. The `governance`
pubkey passed in is the long-lived withdrawal authority (e.g. a
multisig / DAO key) — it may differ from the deployer's upgrade key.
Seeds: `["protocol_fees"]` — singleton.
Authorization: signer must equal the program's upgrade authority,
verified by reading the program's ProgramData PDA
(`upgrade_authority_address`). Prevents anyone from calling this ix
and pinning their own governance key on a fresh deploy.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Upgrade authority of this program. Pays rent + signs.

accounts.name: program
docs: This program's executable account — used to derive ProgramData PDA.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: ProgramData PDA — verifies `authority` is the actual upgrade authority.

instructions.name: init_treasury
docs: Atomically create the TreasuryAccount + PolicyAccount PDA pair bound
to a CompanyAccount. Per design doc §6, both accounts are initialized
together so a company never exists in a state where treasury is set
but policy is not (or vice versa).
Seeds:
• treasury: `["treasury", company_pda]`
• policy:   `["policy",   company_pda]`
Phase 1 defaults (per §2 + §3.2):
• treasury.accepted_assets = `[SOL_PSEUDO_MINT]` — SPL mints added
later via Privileged-class `set_policy`.
• policy budgets = empty (nothing disbursable until operator calls
`set_policy`).
• policy.privileged_threshold_lamports = `u64::MAX` — secondary
signer never required by default.
• policy.agent_operating_fee_bps = `DEFAULT_AGENT_OPERATING_FEE_BPS`
• policy.current_period_anchor = 0 — lazy-initialized on first
disbursement.
Authorization model (M5): canonical invocation is via CPI from
`registry::create_company`. Direct calls remain technically allowed
— there is no controlling-authority check here because Anchor does
not flush `Account<T>` mutations to the underlying data buffer until
handler exit, so reading `company.owner` mid-CPI returns stale bytes.
The omitted check is *safe* because:
1. Defaults written by this ix are identical regardless of caller.
2. PDA seeds pin treasury+policy to the company; re-init is impossible.
3. All subsequent operations (`set_policy`, `disburse_*`,
`register_*`) verify controlling_authority against `company.owner`.
The worst an attacker can do is burn rent (~0.005 SOL) to pre-init a
stranger's treasury with the same defaults the legitimate owner would
have gotten anyway.

accounts.name: company
docs: CompanyAccount this treasury+policy bind to. Program-owner match
enforced — must be a real Registry-owned account, not a forgery.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent for both PDAs. Direct call: operator hot wallet. CPI from
`registry::create_company`: the same `payer` account from registry's
outer ix is forwarded through.

instructions.name: register_company_operations
docs: Register a company-scoped operations key (Disbursement | Anchor).
Privileged class — signed by controlling authority (= company.owner).
Seeds: `["operations", company_pda, &[kind.as_byte()]]`.
Up to 2 per company: one Disbursement, one Anchor (§3.3).
Phase 1 scope: company-scoped only. `register_agent_operations` is
deferred to Phase 2+ per design §2 — per-agent operations keys are
not needed until marketplace settlement.
`action_whitelist`: 8-byte Anchor instruction discriminators allowed
to be signed by this key. Caller is responsible for computing them
off-chain (e.g. `sha256("global:disburse_routine")[0..8]`). Expected
values per design §3.3:
• Disbursement: `[disburse_routine]`
• Anchor:       `[commit_daily_anchor]` (lives in Registry program)
`expiry_unix`: `0` for no expiry, otherwise must be in the future.
`rate_limit_per_period`: max signatures per calendar month (lazily
rolled over inside the ix being signed, in M4+).

accounts.name: company
docs: `controlling_authority` signer check happens in handler.

accounts.name: controlling_authority
docs: Controlling Authority — must equal `CompanyAccount.owner`.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent. Typically the operator hot wallet.

instructions.name: revoke_operations
docs: Mark an OperationsAccount revoked. Terminal flag — once set, no
further signatures via this account are accepted (M4+ disburse
handlers reject `ops.revoked == true`). Privileged class.
Rotation flow: `revoke_operations` → `close_operations` (refunds
rent) → `register_company_operations` with the new signer pubkey.

accounts.name: company
docs: `controlling_authority` signer check happens in handler.

instructions.name: set_governance
docs: Rotate the `governance` withdrawal authority to a new key. Signed by
the CURRENT governance authority — the only way to change `governance`
after init, which is otherwise immutable.
Defends against permanent fund-lock / key compromise: if the
governance key is being retired, moved to a multisig, or is feared
leaked, the current holder rotates to a fresh key here. An attacker
cannot call this without already holding the current key (which would
also let them withdraw), so rotation adds no new theft surface — it is
purely defensive.
`new_governance` cannot be the default pubkey: that would re-lock the
account with no valid signer.

accounts.name: governance
docs: Current governance authority. Must sign to rotate.

instructions.name: set_policy
docs: Update policy fields and/or treasury's accepted-asset list.
Privileged class (§4.2 + §7) — signed by controlling authority.
Each field in `params` is `Option<T>`: `None` = no change, `Some(v)`
= set. `secondary_signer` is `Option<Option<Pubkey>>` so the caller
can distinguish "leave unchanged" (outer `None`), "clear" (outer
`Some(None)`), and "set" (outer `Some(Some(pk))`).
Validation: fee bps ≤ 10_000; vec lengths ≤ MAX_*; mint uniqueness
inside any single vec. Period rollover is NOT performed here — spent
counters are only touched by disbursement instructions (M4+).

accounts.name: company
docs: CompanyAccount this policy/treasury belong to.

accounts.name: controlling_authority
docs: Controlling Authority — must equal `CompanyAccount.owner`.

instructions.name: update_operations_capability
docs: Modify an OperationsAccount's whitelist / rate limit / expiry without
rotating the signer pubkey. Privileged class.
Each field of `params` is `Option<T>`: `None` = leave unchanged.
To rotate the `signer` pubkey, the operator must `revoke_operations`
→ `close_operations` → `register_company_operations` with the new
pubkey. This forces an audit trail across pubkey rotation events.
Allowed on expired ops (operator can extend expiry to revive).
Rejected on revoked ops (terminal state — must close + recreate).

accounts.name: company
docs: `controlling_authority` signer check happens in handler.

instructions.name: withdraw_protocol_fees
docs: Withdraw accumulated protocol fees from the singleton
`ProtocolFeeAccount` to a governance-chosen destination. Without this
instruction, fees credited by the `disburse_*` handlers would be
write-only — permanently locked on the PDA.
Authorization: signed by the `governance` authority pinned at
`init_protocol_fee_account` (e.g. a DAO / multisig key), distinct from
the program's upgrade authority.
Phase 1: SOL only (`mint == SOL_PSEUDO_MINT`), matching the
disbursement path. The withdrawn `amount` is decremented from the
tracked per-asset `balances` entry (accounting truth — cannot withdraw
more than was accumulated) and the PDA's rent-exempt minimum is
protected so the runtime never GCs the account. Partial withdrawals
are allowed.

accounts.name: governance
docs: Governance withdrawal authority pinned at init. Must sign.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Destination for the withdrawn lamports. Governance signs, so it picks
freely (DAO treasury, multisig, etc.).

instructions.name: withdraw_protocol_fees_spl
docs: Withdraw accumulated SPL protocol fees from the singleton
`ProtocolFeeAccount`'s ATA to a governance-chosen destination ATA. SPL
sibling of `withdraw_protocol_fees`. Signed by `governance`; the
withdrawn `amount` is decremented from the tracked per-mint `balances`
(accounting truth — cannot withdraw more than was accumulated) and moved
via a `transfer_checked` CPI signed by the ProtocolFeeAccount PDA. The
destination ATA is created if missing (governance pays rent). Partial
withdrawals allowed.

accounts.name: governance
docs: Governance withdrawal authority pinned at init. Must sign; also pays
rent if the destination ATA is created on demand.

accounts.name: destination
docs: Destination wallet — owner of `destination_token_account`. Governance
picks freely (DAO treasury, multisig, etc.).

accounts.name: fee_token_account
docs: ProtocolFeeAccount PDA's ATA for `mint` — the source of withdrawn fees.

accounts.name: destination_token_account
docs: Destination ATA — created if missing (governance pays rent).

errors.name: CompanyOwnerMismatch
errors.msg: company account is not owned by the Registry program

errors.name: InvalidCompanyAccount
errors.msg: company account data is malformed or too short

errors.name: IncompatibleCompanyVersion
errors.msg: company account version is not supported by this program

errors.name: InvalidProgramData
errors.msg: ProgramData account does not match this program

errors.name: NotUpgradeAuthority
errors.msg: signer is not the program's upgrade authority

errors.name: UnauthorizedSigner
errors.msg: signer is not authorized for this instruction

errors.name: PolicyMismatch
errors.msg: policy/treasury does not match company

errors.name: TooManyEntries
errors.msg: vector exceeds maximum length

errors.name: DuplicateMint
errors.msg: duplicate mint in vector

errors.name: InvalidFeeBps
errors.msg: fee bps exceeds 10000 (100%)

errors.name: InvalidSigner
errors.msg: operations account signer cannot be the default pubkey

errors.name: EmptyWhitelist
errors.msg: operations account requires at least one whitelisted action

errors.name: ExpiryInPast
errors.msg: expiry timestamp must be in the future or zero

errors.name: OperationsRevoked
errors.msg: operations account is revoked

errors.name: AlreadyRevoked
errors.msg: operations account is already revoked

errors.name: NotRevoked
errors.msg: operations account must be revoked before closing

errors.name: WrongOperationsKind
errors.msg: operations account kind does not match instruction expectation

errors.name: OperationsExpired
errors.msg: operations account is past its expiry

errors.name: DiscriminatorNotWhitelisted
errors.msg: instruction discriminator is not whitelisted on operations account

errors.name: RateLimitExceeded
errors.msg: rate limit for operations account is exhausted this period

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: disbursement amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: SplNotSupported
errors.msg: SPL token disbursement is not yet supported (Phase 1 SOL only)

errors.name: AssetNotAllowListed
errors.msg: mint is not in treasury accepted_assets list

errors.name: AssetNotBudgeted
errors.msg: no budget configured for this asset

errors.name: BudgetExceeded
errors.msg: budget remaining is insufficient for this disbursement

errors.name: InsufficientFunds
errors.msg: treasury balance is insufficient (rent-exempt minimum protected)

errors.name: ArithmeticOverflow
errors.msg: arithmetic overflow

errors.name: InvalidDeploymentAccount
errors.msg: deployment account data is malformed or too short

errors.name: IncompatibleDeploymentVersion
errors.msg: deployment account version is not supported by this program

errors.name: DeploymentCompanyMismatch
errors.msg: deployment does not belong to this company

errors.name: DestinationMismatch
errors.msg: destination does not match deployment.receiving_address

errors.name: ReceivingAddressUnset
errors.msg: deployment.receiving_address has not been set

errors.name: SecondarySignerRequired
errors.msg: disbursement above threshold requires secondary signer

errors.name: SecondarySignerMismatch
errors.msg: provided secondary signer does not match policy.secondary_signer

errors.name: MissingDeploymentForAgentDestination
errors.msg: agent destination flag set but no deployment account provided

errors.name: InsufficientFeeBalance
errors.msg: protocol fee balance is insufficient for this withdrawal

errors.name: InvalidGovernance
errors.msg: new governance authority cannot be the default pubkey

errors.name: WrongAssetClass
errors.msg: wrong asset class for this instruction (SPL ix requires a real mint; use the SOL variant for native SOL)

errors.name: MintAccountMismatch
errors.msg: mint account does not match the mint argument

fields.name: mint
docs: SPL mint pubkey — `SOL_PSEUDO_MINT` for SOL.

fields.name: amount
docs: Amount in lamports (SOL) or base units (SPL).

fields.name: kind
docs: What kind of operations key this is (Disbursement | Anchor).
Also encoded into the PDA seed so each company can host one of each.

fields.name: signer
docs: Pubkey of the registered wallet:
• Disbursement: operator-held only — OCCA never holds the privkey.
• Anchor: shared session key — operator + OCCA both hold privkey,
either can sign `commit_daily_anchor` (capability-scoped).

fields.name: action_whitelist
docs: Whitelist of allowed instruction discriminators (8-byte Anchor
discriminators). Defaults set at registration:
• Disbursement: `[disburse_routine]`
• Anchor:       `[commit_daily_anchor]` (lives in Registry program)

fields.name: current_period_anchor
docs: Unix timestamp of start-of-current-month UTC. `0` = uninitialized.

fields.name: expiry_unix
docs: Hard expiry (unix timestamp). `0` = no expiry.

fields.name: revoked
docs: Once true, all future signatures via this account are rejected.
Rotation flow: revoke → close → re-create with new `signer`.

fields.name: routine_budget_per_month
docs: Max disbursable per calendar month via Routine class (auto-signed by
OperationsAccount[Disbursement]).

fields.name: discretionary_budget_per_month
docs: Max disbursable per calendar month via Discretionary class
(operator-signed, e.g. ad-hoc agent payouts).

fields.name: privileged_threshold_lamports
docs: Privileged disbursements above this lamport amount require both
controlling authority + secondary signer. Default `u64::MAX` (no
threshold trip until operator opts in).

fields.name: privileged_threshold_per_token
docs: Per-SPL-token equivalent of `privileged_threshold_lamports`. Empty
vec = no per-token threshold configured.

fields.name: secondary_signer
docs: Secondary signer pubkey. `None` = unconfigured; any threshold-crossing
privileged ix will reject until set.

fields.name: agent_operating_fee_bps
docs: Fee in basis points (300 = 3%). Mutable via `set_policy`. Routes to
`ProtocolFeeAccount` when destination is a `Deployment.receiving_address`
under the same company.

fields.name: current_period_anchor
docs: Unix timestamp of start-of-current-month UTC. `0` = uninitialized;
first disbursement after init lazy-rolls to current month start.

fields.name: governance
docs: Long-lived withdrawal authority — typically a DAO / multisig key,
distinct from the program's upgrade authority. Set at init, then
rotatable by the current holder via `set_governance` (guards against
key loss / compromise). A multisig here survives a single lost key.

fields.name: balances
docs: Per-asset accumulated fee balances.

types.name: SetPolicyParams
docs: Parameter bag for `set_policy`. Each field is `Option<T>`: `None` =
"leave unchanged", `Some(v)` = "set to v". `secondary_signer` is doubly
optional (outer = include in update; inner = new value, with `None` =
clear and `Some(pk)` = set).

fields.name: accepted_assets
docs: Token mints allow-listed for inflow/outflow accounting. SOL is
represented as `SOL_PSEUDO_MINT` (lamports custodied directly on this
PDA). SPL mints are custodied via per-mint Associated Token Accounts
authored by this PDA.

types.name: UpdateOperationsCapabilityParams
docs: Parameter bag for `update_operations_capability`. `None` = leave field
unchanged. To set `expiry_unix` to "no expiry", pass `Some(0)` (sentinel).
